title: prunus × lannesiana f. subfusca
intro: Prunus × lannesiana f. subfusca, also known as the Chinese cherry or black cherry, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that is native to China and is widely cultivated for its ornamental value, as well as for its medicinal and edible properties. The bark, leaves, and flowers of the Prunus × lannesiana f. subfusca plant have been used in traditional Chinese medicine for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The health benefits of Prunus × lannesiana f. subfusca include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce cholesterol levels, and act as an antiseptic to prevent the growth of bacteria and fungi. Prunus × lannesiana f. subfusca can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or syrup, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine as a decoction to treat various health conditions.
